ops.title: 2026-07-16 fleet re-migration onto modular cc-skills fragments — 22 done, 3 deferred (61b5448b), 8 bespoke (ee0ea525); shared prose now edits ONLY in cc-skills plugin/guides/md; migrate-runbook gate lessons
ops.body:
The 2026-07-16 fleet sweep (cc-context session 60dfd1d8) re-migrated the repo fleet onto the modular cc-skills shared fragments at canonical cc-skills sha `7427f102` — full fleet table and per-repo commits in cc-notes note `aa4cb717` (cc-context store). 22 repos migrated with Guides CI green; captain-hook, cc-steer, cc-transcript deferred (trees held by concurrent sessions — backlog task 61b5448b, re-dispatch recipe inside); 8 bespoke-layout repos skipped pending a per-repo decision (task ee0ea525). Consequence: generic guide prose (ask-before-assuming, code-review-response, parallelize, writing-plans, ccx, version-control) now edits ONLY in cc-skills `plugin/guides/md/` — a local fragment carrying that prose in any migrated repo is a regression, not a convention.

**Why:** the 07-09 migration froze shared prose into per-repo fragments one day before cc-skills published the modular pack, creating fleet-wide bidirectional drift (19 repos still had the ancient `**Git.**` paragraph).

**How to apply (migrate-runbook gates, hard-won):** superseded-sentence whitelists need per-repo VARIANTS verbatim (punctuation/flag drift breaks exact matching), and a sentence whitelisted at span level can re-fail at the artifact-diff level in its split form; a content_lost hit on CLAUDE.md may be an old-pin upgrade — check `git -C cc-skills show <old-pin>:plugin/guides/md/claude-rules.md` before treating it as loss; shared-tree collisions were 6/6 of the abort causes — domain-scope dirty-tree gates to `.claude/fragments/**` + lock artifacts, resolve TRUNK in preflight, ship with explicit `--bookmark <trunk>`. git-only repos (docker-dsl, slop-cop) do NOT adopt `cc-skills:version-control` (it asserts colocated jj). Related: [[cc-guides-fragment-pipeline]], [[ship-auto-rebase]].
